hero.description: TravelFlow turns destination ideas into a route your group can actually react to. Draft the itinerary with AI, shape it on a live map and timeline, then share a link that still feels polished when decisions get real.
proof: Drag the route until it feels right
Share one clean link before the group chat gets chaotic

globe.accessibility: 출발 지점에서 전 세계의 다채로운 여행 아이디어로 이어지는 경로를 보여주는 인터랙티브 글로브 미리보기입니다.
globe.fallbackDescription: 라이브 글로브가 없어도 여행 경로, 트립 카드, 출발 지점을 여기서 계속 볼 수 있습니다.

cards.title: 피지 허니문
cards.detail: 해변과 보트 이동이 있는 느린 일정.

bento.title: The pieces of trip planning finally stay in one place
bento.subtitle: Instead of jumping between docs, maps, screenshots, and half-finished chat threads, TravelFlow keeps the route, timing, and trip context moving together.

items.title: Start from a route, not a blank page
items.description: Describe the trip you want in plain language and get a believable first itinerary with city flow, pacing, and activity context already connected.
items.detail: A first draft your group can react to immediately

items.title: Refine the plan without breaking it
items.description: Stretch a stay, reorder a city, or change the pace. TravelFlow keeps the map and timeline aligned so every edit still feels coherent.
items.detail: Map and timing stay in sync

items.title: Borrow proven ideas and make them yours
items.description: Use community-style routes and inspiration as a smart starting point instead of rebuilding a believable trip from scratch.

items.title: Share something your travel crew can understand fast
items.description: Send one link with the route, the pacing, and the notes already in place so feedback happens on the actual trip, not on disconnected screenshots.

items.title: Leave with a plan that still feels polished
items.description: When the route is ready, the practical context is still there: booking links, notes, and a cleaner print-friendly version for the final handoff.
items.detail: Ready to share, save, or print

workflow.title: One flow for drafting, shaping, and sharing
workflow.subtitle: TravelFlow works best when you want the exciting part of planning and the practical part of planning to live in the same place.

steps.title: Start with a destination, a vibe, or a copied route
steps.description: Begin from a country list, a seasonal idea, or a travel concept that is still fuzzy. You do not need to know the perfect sequence yet.

steps.title: Shape the pacing with the map and timeline together
steps.description: See the route on the globe, then tighten the trip on the timeline until it feels realistic for transfers, energy, and group preferences.

steps.title: Share a version that still looks intentional
steps.description: When it is time to get feedback or book, the same plan already has the map, the structure, and the extra context people usually ask for.

glance.title: A plan that already looks share-ready
glance.description: Maps, day-by-day timing, and the practical context stay aligned so reactions come faster and with less confusion.
items: Live route view with pacing context
Booking-ready activity links when you need them
A cleaner print layout for the final version

cta.badge: Build it while the idea is still fresh
cta.title: Start with the route. Keep the travel mood.
cta.subtitle: Draft your next trip in minutes, then keep refining it in the same place instead of rebuilding it across five tools.
